pi_items.source: Direct file inspection at /workspace/data/OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt; file exists and is 2.9MB with proper OpenBCI format
Verified through export_result.sh and task description; path matches expected output location
OpenBCI Documentation: https://docs.openbci.com/Software/OpenBCISoftware/GUIWidgets/; confirmed as official widget that displays brain activity topography as heatmap
OpenBCI Documentation confirms Band Power widget exists; provides visualization of relative voltages across frequency bands

Verified through multiple sources: OpenBCI docs list Alpha as 8-13 Hz; neuroscience literature confirms Alpha frequency band is 8-13 Hz (https://www.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/books/NBK539805/, https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Alpha_wave)
Task description specifies 8-13 Hz for Alpha band; confirmed by OpenBCI documentation and neuroscience literature (ScienceDirect, NCBI, Wikipedia)

privileged_info_summary: The EEG recording file 'OpenBCI-EEG-S001-EyesOpen.txt' is derived from the publicly available PhysioNet EEG Motor Movement/Imagery Dataset (eegmmidb 1.0.0), containing baseline eyes-open recordings from subject S001 at 250 Hz sample rate with 8 EEG channels. The Head Plot widget is a legitimate OpenBCI GUI visualization component that displays topographic brain activity as a heat map. Band Power is an OpenBCI metric showing relative signal power across frequency bands. Alpha is the frequency band 8-13 Hz, which is the standard neuroscience definition used in OpenBCI. All claimed features (Head Plot widget, Band Power metric, Alpha band) are real and accurately named in the task.
